questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle moves in a straight line with retardation proportional to its displacement. Its loss of kinetic energy for any displacement \(x\) is proportional to:
questions.solution: Retardation \(a = -kx\). We know \(v \frac{dv}{dx} = -kx\). Integrating both sides, \(\frac{v^2}{2} = -\frac{kx^2}{2} + C\). Loss of KE is proportional to \(x^2\).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which of the following compounds will exhibit geometrical isomerism?
questions.solution: 1-Phenyl-2-butene has restricted rotation around the C=C bond and different groups attached to the double-bonded carbons, allowing for cis-trans isomerism.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are the roots of the equation \(x^2 - x + 1 = 0\), then \(\alpha^{2009} + \beta^{2009}\) is equal to:
questions.solution: The roots of \(x^2 - x + 1 = 0\) are \(-\omega\) and \(-\omega^2\). Substituting these into the expression gives \((-\omega)^{2009} + (-\omega^2)^{2009} = -(\omega^2 + \omega) = 1\).
